compositional_truth_check.P1_String_of_Nickels: Present in yellow pot, showing signs of desiccation.
compositional_truth_check.P2_Mexican_Mint: Systemic Loss; no viable mint foliage detected in the black pot.
compositional_truth_check.P3_Pothos: Present (anchor: white rabbit); significant leaf senescence observed.
compositional_truth_check.P4_Silver_Guest: Present (shared in black pot); single leaf remains, showing signs of chlorosis/necrosis.

inventory_reconciliation.notes: The biome is experiencing a rapid decline in photosynthetic capacity. P2 is effectively lost.

plant_audit.P1: Stems are thinning; foliage is sparse and losing turgor.
plant_audit.P2: No active growth; soil surface appears devoid of the registered specimen.
plant_audit.P3: The Pothos leaf is undergoing severe necrosis, transitioning from yellow to brown.
plant_audit.P4: The single remaining leaf is the primary indicator of health; it is currently stable but isolated.

biome_observations.surface_debris: Significant accumulation of organic decay (fallen leaves) and white particulate matter.
biome_observations.incidental_growth: None observed; the environment is currently hostile to secondary germination.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Initial state shows a yellowing Pothos leaf and a single green P4 leaf.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_t2: Progressive browning of the Pothos leaf; P1 foliage appears to retract.
temporal_deltas.t1_to_current: Total collapse of the Pothos leaf structure; P4 leaf remains the only green anchor.

visual_health_inference.inference: The plants are suffering from severe water stress or root-zone failure. The lack of turgor in the Pothos and the loss of the Mexican Mint suggest a failure in the irrigation or substrate moisture retention.

anomalies: Presence of white particulate matter (likely mineral salts or perlite) on the soil surface.
Complete disappearance of P2 (Mexican Mint) from the shared black pot.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection is in a state of advanced senescence. The Pothos (P3) has transitioned from a chlorotic state to a necrotic state over the 5-day observation period. The 'Silver Guest' (P4) is the only specimen maintaining a semblance of structural integrity. The environment is dry, and the lack of new growth suggests a dormant or dying biome.
